description:
About Us:

Informatech is a leading ICT consultancy with over 240 professionals specialising in Technical Services, Testing, Project Delivery, and Cyber Security. Our success is recognised nationally, we were named Sub-Contractor of the Year at the Defence Connect Awards and ranked for a third consecutive year on the AFR's Fast 100 list, which highlights our consistent growth and success.

Location: Mawson Lakes, SA (Onsite)

Security Clearance: Active Negative Vetting 1 (NV1) – Mandatory

We are seeking an experienced Verification & Validation (V&V) Engineer to support a major Defence maritime capability uplift project focused on modernising shipborne electronic warfare systems.

This program is a long-term Defence initiative delivering upgraded, integrated, and scalable electronic warfare capabilities to enhance situational awareness and strengthen control of the electromagnetic spectrum in contested environments. The role will provide systems engineering services across requirements management, integration, verification, and validation activities to support system certification and capability assurance.

Reporting to the Program Manager, you will work within a multidisciplinary engineering team contributing to the delivery of complex software and hardware systems in a high-assurance Defence environment.

Key Responsibilities

Conduct Verification and Validation (V&V) activities, including writing procedures, inspection and analysis reports, and conducting or witnessing demonstration and test events.

Support integration and test activities across complex software and hardware systems.

Develop, deliver, and maintain Verification Cross Reference Matrices (VCRM).

Conduct hardware and software configuration reviews.

Develop and maintain end-to-end requirements traceability across specifications to establish a holistic verification baseline.

Assist in compiling Objective Quality Evidence (OQE) to support formal system certification.

Support management of requirements databases in accordance with the requirements management plan.

Develop and deliver Requests for Problem Resolution (RPRs) to initiate Engineering Change Proposal (ECP) workflows.

Prepare formal test deliverables and artefacts for review and acceptance.

Required Experience/Skills

Minimum 4 years of relevant experience in Verification, Validation, and Integration activities.

Hands-on experience decomposing requirements and maintaining end-to-end traceability (requirement → test → result → defect/evidence) using tools such as DOORS, Cameo, Jira, and Confluence.

Experience in Integration, Verification, and Validation of complex software systems and military hardware.

Ability to capture concise, high-quality verification evidence suitable for formal acceptance.

Strong understanding of systems engineering principles within a Defence environment.

Ability to work effectively within multidisciplinary teams in high-assurance programs.

Desirable Skills and Experience

Software development and automated testing experience.

Engagement Type

ASAP start – 6-month contract with possible extension.

Perks of the role:

$5000 annual training allowance for Permanent staff

Additional 5 days of paid training leave for Permanent staff

Access to industry leading Testing experts who make up our management team

Formal mentoring and buddy programs

A dedicated Service Delivery Manager to assist you in planning your career goals and training courses (paid for by us)

Opportunity to gain exposure to various client sites

$500 annual allowance for anything relating to your Health and Wellbeing

How to apply:

Send us your most up-to-date resume along with authority for exclusive representation.

Let us know if you prefer to be a Permanent Employee or Contractor.

Address the selection criteria (separate document).

Provide details of your preferred payroll company – ITG Payroll or other (If other, include Company Name, ABN, Physical Address and Email Address).

Include security clearance details (CSID Number, Level and Expiry).

Include contact details of 2 referees (only contacted if shortlisted).

Advise of your notice period/availability.

Provide details of upcoming leave dates (exceeding 1 week).
